title: Holocron of Darth Krayt

The comic dates itself to around [[130 ABY]], therefore the holocron must have been created around that [[Standard year/Legends|year]].</ref>

{{Quote|Sometimes, things must be broken in order to be rebuilt. As I have bent the Force to my will, so I will bend the galaxy and it will know order. My true work is about to begin.|Darth Krayt, in his holocron|<ref name="Leg1" />}}
The '''holocron of Darth Krayt''' was a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] created by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] around [[130 ABY]] to store the sum total of his knowledge and beliefs, though it was still incomplete at the height of the [[Second Imperial Civil War|Sith-Imperial War]]. The [[Holocron/Legends|holocron's]] contents included information known only to Krayt himself and a speech he recorded shortly after creating the holocron.

==Description==
{{Quote|I know the Jedi from old. They could never share my vision.|Darth Krayt, in his holocron|<ref name="Leg1" />}}
[[File:DarthKraytEAtlas.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Darth Krayt, the holocron's creator]]
The holocron of Darth Krayt<ref name="Leg1" /> was a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] created by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] of the [[One Sith]]<ref name="LECG" /> around [[130 ABY]],<ref name="DateCreation" /> whose existence was known only to Krayt himself and his aide [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]]. Created to store all of Krayt's knowledge, beliefs and philosophies, Darth Krayt's [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] was a repository of great knowledge and contained secrets and lore known only to Krayt himself. Nevertheless, the holocron was still incomplete as of the height of the [[Second Imperial Civil War|Sith-Imperial War]]<ref name="LECG" />—a conflict that ended around [[138 ABY]].<ref name="War6">[[Legacy — War 6|''Legacy — War'' 6]]</ref>

Among the holocron's contents was a sermon by Krayt recorded around 130 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], outlining the impact of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] invaders on [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], starting with their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], and recounting Krayt and the [[Fel Empire]]'s scheme to destroy the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]. Krayt's speech also mentioned his familiarity with the old [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and how he planned to bend the galaxy to his will.<ref name="Leg1" /> The holocron's contents could be analyzed for signs of weakness and possibly even uncover secret plans if it fell into the possession of the Galactic Alliance or the [[New Jedi Order]]'s [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]]. There was a possibility that it would cause a coup within Krayt's One Sith if his rivals in the order became aware of its existence.<ref name="LECG" />

==History==
{{Quote|Two days ago the Jedi Temple on Coruscant fell. Today, at their academy on Ossus, the last of the Jedi will die.|Darth Krayt, in his holocron|<ref name="Leg1" />}}
The holocron was created by Darth Krayt<ref name="LECG" /> around 130 ABY<ref name="DateCreation" /> after the Sith Lord discovered that his body was in an even worse state than he previously thought.<ref name="LECG" /> A section of the holocron was recorded two [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after Krayt's realization, shortly before the [[Massacre at Ossus|massacre]] of the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Academy]] on<ref name="Leg1" /> the planet<ref name="LECG" /> [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]].<ref name="Leg1" /> Despite its large repository of information, the holocron was not yet complete by the height of the Sith-Imperial War.<ref name="LECG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The holocron of Darth Krayt was first mentioned in the [[Legacy (2006) 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' comic series. The issue was written by [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="Leg1" /> on [[June 21]], [[2006]].<ref name="Date">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54719/star_wars_legacy_2006_1|text=Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 1}}</ref>
